content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


February 15, 2008


Charlene D. Cruz, Section Head
Campaign Finance Disclosure Section
Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder
12400 Imperial Highway
P. O. Box 1024
Norwalk, CA 90651-1024

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-019

Dear Ms. Cruz:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding your duties under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

As the filing officer for campaign statements filed by candidates for county elective offices, are you required to accept the Candidate Intention Statements (Form 501) filed by superior court judges and forward them to the Secretary of State, who is the designated filing officer for campaign reports and statements filed by judges of the superior court?

CONCLUSION

No. You have no obligation to accept and transmit judicial candidate campaign forms to the Secretary of State.

_________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

Our File No. A-08-019
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Act requires individuals seeking or holding elective office, including a
judicial office, to file periodic reports disclosing contributions received and expenditures
made in connection with their elections. (Sections 82020, 82023, and 84100 et seq.) The
first form a candidate or officeholder must file in order to solicit or receive contributions
is the Candidate Intention Statement (Form 501). Section 85200 provides:

"Prior to the solicitation or receipt of any contribution
or loan, an individual who intends to be a candidate for an
elective state office, pursuant to Section 82024, shall file
with the Secretary of State an original statement, signed
under penalty of perjury, of intention to be a candidate for a
specific office.
"An individual who intends to be a candidate for any
other elective office shall file the statement of intention
with the same filing officer and in the same location as the
individual would file an original campaign statement
pursuant to subdivisions (c), (d), and (e) of Section 84215.
"For purposes of this section, 'contribution' and 'loan'
do not include any payments from the candidate's personal
funds for a candidate filing fee or a candidate statement of
qualifications fee."

After filing Form 501, the location for filing other campaign statements by
judicial candidates are, for the most part, specified in Section 84215(b), as follows:2

"(b) Members of the Legislature or Board of
Equalization, court of appeal justices, superior court
judges, candidates for those offices, their controlled
committees, and committees formed or existing primarily
to support or oppose these candidates or officeholders:
(1) The original and one copy with the Secretary of
State.
(2) One copy with the elections official of the county
with the largest number of registered voters in the districts
affected." (Emphasis added.)

Although Section 85200 does not specifically state where candidates for superior
court judge must file Form 501, the clear intent of the statute is that all candidates file

2 The filing location for certain other statements are set out elsewhere in the Act, such as the
Statement of Organization (Form 410) required under Section 84101.

Our File No. A-08-019
Page 3


the form in the same location that they file original campaign statements pursuant to
Section 84215. Therefore, superior court judges and candidates are required to file the
Form 501 (as well as their other original campaign statements) directly with the Secretary
of State. You have no obligation to accept these forms or forward them to another filing
officer.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

CW:jgl
